topic.title: SAP TM Business Processes & Transportation Scenarios

learningObjectives.title: Identify TM transportation scenarios
learningObjectives.description: Understand inbound, outbound, intercompany, returns and multimodal logistics flows.

learningObjectives.title: Explain inbound and outbound processes
learningObjectives.description: Describe process triggers, planning and execution flow in each scenario.

learningObjectives.title: Understand multimodal transportation
learningObjectives.description: Explain how SAP TM plans shipments across multiple transportation modes.

sections.content: SAP Transportation Management supports multiple transportation scenarios including inbound logistics, outbound logistics, intercompany movements, returns processing and multimodal transportation. The system optimizes transportation planning while providing end-to-end visibility.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: The same SAP TM planning engine can manage inbound, outbound and internal transportation using different business documents.

sections.title: Business Scenarios
rows: Goods moving from suppliers to company warehouses.
Goods delivered from company to customers.
Transportation using multiple modes like road, rail and sea.

sections.content: SAP TM supports multimodal transportation where a shipment travels through multiple transportation modes, such as truck to port, ocean vessel to destination country and truck to final customer.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A consumer goods company transports products from its manufacturing plant to a seaport by truck, ships them internationally by ocean vessel and delivers them to retailers using local road carriers. SAP TM manages the complete multimodal process within one transportation plan.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Interviewers often ask the difference between inbound and outbound transportation. Always explain both the business document that starts the process and the business objective.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not assume SAP TM is only used for outbound logistics. It supports inbound, outbound, intercompany, returns and multimodal transportation.

items: SAP TM supports inbound and outbound transportation.
Multimodal transportation combines multiple transport modes.
Freight Units are planned into Freight Orders.
Different business documents trigger different transportation scenarios.
Transportation planning is optimized using SAP TM.
